summary:
The start of the chewing stroke can be variable due to variations in musculature activation and neurological controls; thus, it differs between patients as indicated by both anatomical considerations and functional observations related to jaw movement and mastication.
```

The summary focuses only on two core aspects—muscular variation leading to positional difference and neurologically driven uniqueness—and concludes accordingly without extraneous details. ``` Summary: 
The beginning of the chewing stroke varies among individuals because of differences in how various facial muscles are activated and controlled neurally, making its onset unique per person regardless of standard dental landmarks such as centric relation or centric occlusion.
